title: 2026 Oscar Acting Precursor Scorecard: Best Actress
text: Gold Derby's analysis examines which 2026 Oscar nominees earned nominations across major precursor awards: Golden Globes, Critics Choice, BAFTA, and SAG Actor Awards. Only 11 of the 20 Oscar acting nominees achieved the feat of sweeping all four precursors. In the Best Actress category, three performers secured precursor nominations across all major awards. Jessie Buckley in Hamnet currently leads the category with 97.7% odds to win the Oscar, achieving the complete precursor sweep and positioning herself as the frontrunner for Best Actress. Rose Byrne in If I Had Legs I'd Kick You also swept all precursor awards, with Oscar odds at 1.6%. Emma Stone in Bugonia completed the precursor sweep with 0.3% Oscar winning probability. Kate Hudson in Song Sung Blue came close to the sweep, securing nominations at Golden Globes, BAFTA, SAG, and Actor Awards but only stumbled at the Critics Choice Awards. The article notes that broad precursor support significantly strengthens Oscar prospects, though it remains non-determinative for final Academy recognition.
